Off Calendar Jul-28-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kelley T. Mahoney of POND NORTH LLP (415-217-1240), appearing for defendant / moving party Sears, Roebuck and Co.
Natalie Garcia Lasinsky of POND NORTH LLP (415-217-1240), appearing for defendant / moving party Astra Flooring Company
Kathleen B. Ebrahimi of POND NORTH LLP (415-217-1240), appearing for defendant / moving party Fraser's Boiler Service, Inc.
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Thomas McAllister and Velma McAllister

RULING:
DEFENDANT SEARS, ROEBUCK AND CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES

Prior to the hearing plaintiffs and defendants Sears, Astra Flooring and Fraser's stipulated to incorporate their argument in all three motions into one record. Hearing held and tentative ruling in the Sears, Roebuck's motion adopted: Defendant Sears, Roebuck and Co.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs' claims are barred by the statute of limitations. Defendant demonstrated that Mr. McAllister was disabled in 2005, that his disability was at least contributed to by his exposure to asbestos and that he knew, or through the exercise of reasonable diligence should have known, the causes or contributing factors of his disability more than one year before plaintiffs filed this action. (Uram v. Abex Corp. (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 1425.) Plaintiffs failed to present evidence to create a triable issue whether their claims are barred by the statute of limitations. (Uram v. Abex Corp. (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 1425; Jolly v. Eli Lilly & Co. (1988) 44 Cal.3d 1103.) This ruling makes the other issues raised by defendant's motion moot. Order for Sears, Roebuck signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Tara L. Williams of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13428 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.
Off Calendar Mar-26-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Thomas McAllister and Velma Jean McAllister

RULING:
DEFENDANT BORGWARNER MORSE TEC INC., AS SUCCESSOR-BY-MERGER TO BORG-WARNER CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant BorgWarner Morse Tec Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. McAllister was exposed to a BorgWarner product. Defendant's objection to Mr. McAllister's deposition testimony identifying BorgWarner clutches is overruled. Mr. McAllister's deposition testimony is admissible. (In re Cindy L. (1997) 17 Cal.4th 15.) Mr. McAllister's deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to sustain its initial burden of negating an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. First, when read in conjunction with Mr. McAllister's deposition testimony, the declarations of Carole Cameron, Bryon Frantz and Ted Lorona merely create a triable issue whether the clutches purchased from Linda Vista Auto/Cameron's were BorgWarner. Second, those declarations are insufficient to create an impossibility that the clutches purchased from Linda Vista Auto/Cameron's were BorgWarner. Plaintiffs' objections to the declaration of Carole Cameron in its entirety are overruled and as to paragraph 3 stating that Cameron's was a NAPA store and only carried NAPA products plaintiffs' objections are sustained as to all times outside the 1952-1956 range. Plaintiffs' objections to the declarations of Bryon Frantz and Ted Lorona are overruled. The declarations meet the requirements of CRC 3.1115. Defendant's alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is off calendar because plaintiffs have dismissed such claim. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)
